custodyModel.statement: User funds remain in user-controlled ERC-4626 pools and account-scoped margin accounts. The protocol never takes custody. Privileged powers are limited to parameter governance behind a timelock, emergency pause, and permissioned liquidation keepers. Asset managers can only route through whitelisted protocols via constrained adapters; they cannot withdraw collateral to arbitrary destinations.

TB01.name: Pool
TB01.description: Lender deposits and share accounting. Holds idle and lent capital.

TB02.name: MarginAccount
TB02.description: Per-borrower account holding collateral and debt; can only call whitelisted adapters.

TB03.name: Adapters
TB03.description: Constrained gateways to external protocols. Each new adapter widens the external attack surface.

TB04.name: MarginEngine
TB04.description: Off-chain portfolio risk engine. Produces advisory signals only; never authoritative over solvency.

TB05.name: Governance
TB05.description: Multisig and timelock controlling parameters and upgrades.

Audit1.scope: Core: pool, credit manager, margin account, two to three adapters, liquidation.

Audit2.scope: Margin-engine integration points, additional adapters, multi-chain.

Audit3.scope: Permissionless and retail surface plus public audit contest.

T01.title: Oracle price manipulation
T01.mitigation: Chainlink feeds with staleness and deviation guards; collateral haircuts; stress-tested value used for health.

T02.title: Unauthorized external call through an adapter
T02.mitigation: WhitelistRegistry of protocols, assets, and selectors; account-scoped routing; adapters cannot move funds to arbitrary destinations.

T03.title: Failure to liquidate an unhealthy account
T03.mitigation: Permissioned keepers with watchdog; on-chain liquidation floor independent of the off-chain engine; private submission to resist censorship.

T04.title: Pool share inflation (first-depositor attack)
T04.mitigation: ERC-4626 virtual shares and decimals offset; minimum initial deposit seeded at deployment.

T05.title: Privileged role compromise
T05.mitigation: Safe multisig plus timelock on parameters and upgrades; guardian limited to pause; minimal admin surface.

T06.title: Reentrancy across adapter calls
T06.mitigation: Checks-effects-interactions, reentrancy guards, and a single health check enforced after the full multicall.

T07.title: Margin-engine signal spoofing
T07.mitigation: Engine signals are advisory; the on-chain floor is authoritative for solvency; signed and authenticated feeds.

T08.title: Disputed privileged action without audit trail
T08.mitigation: All privileged actions are timelocked and emit events; an off-chain audit log records actor, action, and timestamp.
